title: Fix install.sh false-positive hard-fail: classify drift_detail so extra-file-only drift (versions matching) doesn't abort the installer
action: scripts/install.sh's final verify step (~487-498) hard-fails whenever s.onboarding.drift !== false, with no distinction between a real version/hash mismatch and an unmanaged extra .mjs sitting in .bee/bin/lib/ (computeRuntimeDrift's file-set check, skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs:461-508). bee.mjs status --json already exposes per-item detail as s.onboarding.drift_detail (populated whenever drift_detail.length > 0): hash-mismatch/missing entries are bare relative paths (optionally suffixed " (missing)"), extra-file entries are suffixed " (extra)". Rewrite install.sh's verify node snippet (the block piped after printf '%s' "$STATUS" | node -e '...') to: keep the existing bee_version/plugin_version/expected equality check fatal exactly as today; when drift is true, inspect drift_detail -- if every entry ends with " (extra)" (and versions already matched expected), do NOT hard-fail on that alone: print an actionable message naming the extra file path(s) and instructing removal (or noting they self-heal on next onboarding refresh), then continue to the up_to_date recheck step below it; if any entry lacks the " (extra)" suffix (a real hash mismatch or missing-file entry), keep the existing hard-fail with the existing 'version parity failed' message. Add a regression test to scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs mirroring the file's existing check()-based style: install a target to a clean up-to-date state, then plant one unmanaged extra .mjs file directly in <target>/.bee/bin/lib/ (a name absent from onboarding.json's managed.lib), re-run the installer's verify path, and assert the run does NOT hard-fail / exits successfully, while the output names the planted extra file. Do not touch skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs -- drift_detail is already exposed; no bee.mjs source change is needed for this fix. Because scripts/install.sh is a release-manifest-hashed root, this cell also carries the regen obligation: after the code+test change, run (in order) node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs, node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply, then node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write, and include the regenerated docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json in the diff.

truths: install.sh's verify step hard-fails on any drift_detail entry that lacks the " (extra)" suffix (real hash-mismatch or missing-file drift), or on any bee_version/plugin_version mismatch against expected -- unchanged from today

when drift is true solely from one or more " (extra)"-suffixed drift_detail entries and versions already match expected, verify does not hard-fail on that alone and continues to the up_to_date recheck step
in that extra-file-only case, the verify step's output names the specific extra file path(s) so a human/agent knows what to clear

a new regression test in scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs covers: target already at expected version, one unmanaged extra .mjs planted in .bee/bin/lib/, asserts the run succeeds (no hard-fail) and the output names the planted file
existing tests in scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs stay green, including the clean-install "status must report no drift" assertion (~line 316) and the malformed-plugin-list/shape-drift tests
release manifest regenerated via render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs -> onboard_bee.mjs --apply -> release_manifest.mjs --write, and release_manifest.mjs --check passes
prohibitions: never silently swallow a real hash-mismatch or missing-file drift entry -- those keep hard-failing exactly as before
never widen the check to tolerate a bee_version/plugin_version mismatch -- those stay fatal exactly as today
no change to skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs -- drift_detail already exists, fix is confined to install.sh's consumption of it plus the new test

trace.outcome: install.sh's final verify step now treats extra-file-only drift (unmanaged .mjs in .bee/bin/lib/, versions already matching) as a named warning instead of a hard-fail, while real hash-mismatch/missing/version drift still hard-fails exactly as before; added a red-first regression test proving this in scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs; regenerated the release manifest for the two hashed roots.
trace.verify_output:
test_installers_e2e (bash): 24 passed, 0 failed
release_manifest --check: 510 file(s) match stored manifest
